title: FormalSpec
description: Structured specification format richer than GitHub issue templates. Provides structured acceptance criteria, dependency declarations, risk pre-classification, and machine-verifiable completion conditions. Designed for Phase 5e Spec-Driven Interface — enables agents to verify implementation completeness programmatically.

spec_version.description: Schema version for this formal spec format.

description.description: Detailed description of the work to be performed.

origin.description: How this spec was created. human: manually authored. runtime-signal: generated from a runtime DI. goals-item: derived from GOALS.md. retrospective: identified during post-merge retrospective.

issue_ref.description: Reference to the corresponding GitHub issue (e.g., '#163'). Null if no issue exists yet.

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured acceptance criteria. Each criterion has a testable assertion and a verification method.

dependencies.description: Explicit dependency declarations for this spec.

risk_classification.description: Pre-classified risk assessment for this spec.

severity.description: Expected risk severity of the change.

policy_profile.description: Governance policy profile applicable to this change.

affected_layers.description: Governance layers affected by this change.

compliance_flags.description: Compliance frameworks relevant to this change.

completion_conditions.description: Machine-verifiable conditions that must all be true for the spec to be considered complete.

scope.description: Explicit scope boundaries for the change.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded work — prevents scope creep.

panels_required.description: Governance panels that must evaluate this change.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this spec was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last update to this spec.

author.description: Author of this spec (human name, agent persona, or 'system').

acceptance_criterion.description: A single acceptance criterion with a testable assertion.

description.description: Human-readable description of what must be true.

verification_method.description: How this criterion is verified. automated_test: a test suite asserts it. schema_validation: JSON Schema validates the output. file_exists: a specific file must exist. grep_match: a pattern must match in specified files. manual_review: requires human confirmation. panel_emission: a panel must emit a passing verdict.

verification_target.description: Target for automated verification — file path, test name, schema path, or grep pattern depending on verification_method.

priority.description: MoSCoW priority. must: blocks completion. should: expected but negotiable. could: nice to have.

dependency.description: A dependency this spec has on another artifact or condition.

type.description: blocking: must be resolved before work starts. non-blocking: should be resolved but work can proceed. informational: awareness only.

ref.description: Reference to the dependency — issue number, spec_id, file path, or external URL.

resolved.description: Whether this dependency has been resolved.

completion_condition.description: A machine-verifiable condition for spec completion.

assertion.description: The specific assertion to verify — file path, schema reference, grep pattern, check name, or panel name depending on type.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this condition verifies.
